--- Thread (50 messages, 2019-01-23) ---
[2019-01-23 12:08] winged: Prismata borrows strong mechanical motifs from:
1) **RTSs**: a focus on managing an economy vs building an army to punish your opponent. Resource management. Unit groups. Build orders.
[2019-01-23 12:09] winged: Not only does it have three factions, it has the _original_ three factions: clear Zerg / Terran / Protoss analogues
[2019-01-23 12:12] winged: 2) **CCGs**: total information "board states" with geographic distance/proximity completely abstracted away into one large fight space. Units that are represented by artwork + numeric stats + rules text boxes. Units that can be "tapped".
[2019-01-23 12:14] winged: 3) **Deckbuilders**: units from limited supply pools. Randomly constructed markets every new game. Constructing economic engines informed by the clearly signaled economic engines of other players
[2019-01-23 12:14] Deleted User (Deleted User): isn't that just an economy/attack game
[2019-01-23 12:14] Deleted User (Deleted User): I would sooner call pris a TBS
[2019-01-23 12:14] winged: 4) **Abstract strategy games**: determinism, total information, near perfect symmetry
[2019-01-23 12:15] winged: Honestly I think "TBS" is one of the weakest connecting lines, unless you think of chess and go as TBSs
[2019-01-23 12:16] winged: There's a difference between deck building econ / econ vs RTS econ / attack, and Prismata has elements of both
[2019-01-23 12:16] Deleted User (Deleted User): I would call it a TBS econ/attack
[2019-01-23 12:16] winged: A lot of people don't? At least I don't. TBS to me refers to, like, XCOM and Civilization and stuff
[2019-01-23 12:17] Deleted User (Deleted User): well like isn't pris more similar to xcom/civ than a RTS
[2019-01-23 12:17] awaclus: I don't think Pris is very similar to Civ?
[2019-01-23 12:18] winged: I'm talking about mechanical motifs specifically. I can't think of any XCOM/civ style games that carry strategic isomorphisms with Prismata
[2019-01-23 12:18] awaclus: I haven't played XCOM and I haven't played all that much Civ either but it didn't really seem like it had all that much in common with Prismata
[2019-01-23 12:18] winged: My entire point is that it's not enough to say "but it has turns and strategy so therefore it's turn based strategy"
[2019-01-23 12:19] winged: There's also a term for chess/go and that's abstract strategy
[2019-01-23 12:19] awaclus: I can certainly see why people like to call it RTS with caveats
[2019-01-23 12:19] Deleted User (Deleted User): ah so that's easy pris is an abstract strategy game
[2019-01-23 12:19] winged: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_strategy_game
Abstract strategy game
An abstract strategy game is a strategy game in which the theme is not important to the experience of playing. Many of the world's classic board games, including chess, Go (also called "wei-chi" and "baduk"), checkers and draughts, xiangqi (Chinese chess), shogi (Japanese ver...
[2019-01-23 12:19] awaclus: but it isn't an abstract strategy game :NotLikeThis:
[2019-01-23 12:19] Deleted User (Deleted User): ah pris is an abstract strategy game if kids ignore the theme
[2019-01-23 12:20] winged: It's not "easy", there are reasons why prismata is compared to all of these genres
[2019-01-23 12:20] winged: The purpose of genre is not to find the One True Category
[2019-01-23 12:20] winged: It's to communicate intent and feel to communities of players
[2019-01-23 12:20] Deleted User (Deleted User): To me, many TBSes feel like RTSes
[2019-01-23 12:21] winged: "this game is like an RTS!" is meaningful because it's trying to say "it carries a lot of the same core engagements I loved from StarCraft!"
[2019-01-23 12:21] winged: "Prismata is clearly an X and everyone else is wrong" kinda just skips a lot of potential markets
[2019-01-23 12:21] Deleted User (Deleted User): I get the utility, but the only reason it has utility is cause TBS is criminally underused
[2019-01-23 12:21] awaclus: to me, many TBHs feel like CBAs
[2019-01-23 12:22] winged: That's because TBS is criminally underdefined as a full thematic genre
[2019-01-23 12:22] Deleted User (Deleted User): i would call TBS a turn based RTS
[2019-01-23 12:23] winged: Games included in TBS:
1) civilization
2) XCOM
3) fire emblem
4) armello
[2019-01-23 12:23] winged: Only one is even close to an RTS
[2019-01-23 12:23] winged: People don't use the word "TBS" because the word "TBS" can mean practically anything
[2019-01-23 12:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): i assume fire emblem is like advance wars right
[2019-01-23 12:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): surely you can make an RTS out of that
[2019-01-23 12:24] winged: If a term equally refers to fire emblem and civilization, it's not a very communicative term by itself
[2019-01-23 12:24] winged: There is no economy management in fire emblem
[2019-01-23 12:24] winged: It's strictly campaign based using named units
[2019-01-23 12:24] winged: No bases, no money, no builds
[2019-01-23 12:24] siepu: fire emblem is tactical rpg I guess
[2019-01-23 12:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): it feels fucking weird calling a TBS an RTS tho
[2019-01-23 12:24] winged: You just show up with ten guys and try to leave with ten guys
[2019-01-23 12:25] siepu: and yes Prismata is TBS but I use 'turn based starcraft with random available units'
[2019-01-23 12:25] winged: That's what mechanical motifs are about. Like. Here:
[2019-01-23 12:26] winged: https://youtu.be/2TGT9k9YewM
https://youtu.be/fuZFk1hOxjo

[2019-01-23 12:26] winged: But it's also super duper an FPS
[2019-01-23 12:26] winged: Not just any FPS: it's Quake

[2019-01-23 19:23] amalloy: @Wilson in the 2020 elections for moderator my platform will be deleting every message that refers to prismata units as sexual entities
[2019-01-23 19:23] winged: I'm really not a fan of minority sexual identities as punchlines to jokes
[2019-01-23 19:24] winged: or even worse, pejoratives
[2019-01-23 19:25] amalloy: yes. it is really unfriendly. most people respect that but there is a small group of prismata players who do this often. i wish they would stop
[2019-01-23 19:26] winged: I kinda poked anonymous about it. I'm glad it's not just me
[2019-01-23 19:26] winged: I really don't want to invest in a community that treats people like me and my friends like this?
[2019-01-23 19:29] deadhour: What's the reason for Shadowfang being 10 supply but Cynestra being only 4?
[2019-01-23 19:30] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): cynestra deals 3 damage, 3dmg units all have 4 supply
[2019-01-23 19:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): also shadowfang wouldn't be a credible threat at 4 supply
[2019-01-23 19:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): in that if it had 4 supplies and you were going big blue, if opponent goes AA you dont have to even pressure it, just be like "okay you get your 4 fangs"
[2019-01-23 19:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): generally with big red it's supposed to be something you have to pressure
[2019-01-23 19:31] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): I think 4 supply fang would be stronger than 10 supply fang. Because people would go for it less often and win more games :LongNoseLiar:
[2019-01-23 19:31] deadhour: That's something I hadn't noticed
[2019-01-23 19:32] deadhour: Though not entirely accurate since Bombarder is 3 Attack and 10 supply
[2019-01-23 19:32] deadhour: But I assume you meant all units that provide 3 permanent damage are 4 supply
[2019-01-23 19:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): cluster bolt is 4 attack and 10 supply :EGGERS:
[2019-01-23 19:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): venge cannon is 5 attack and 9 supply :LongNoseLiar:
[2019-01-23 19:32] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): cluster gives your opponent a charge too so it's basically 3 attack 😛
[2019-01-23 19:33] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): venge is 2 attack, venge and 4 conduits are 5 attack
[2019-01-23 19:33] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): 4 conduits only have 2.5 supply
[2019-01-23 19:33] awaclus: >yes. it is really unfriendly.
I don't think it's unfriendly, I think it's respectful
[2019-01-23 19:33] deadhour: At the 5 attack isn't permanent
[2019-01-23 19:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): but wait, scorch is 3 permanent attack and 10 supply
[2019-01-23 19:35] awaclus: I would feel alienated if people didn't make fun of me in a lighthearted way
[2019-01-23 19:35] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): scorch is 1 permanent attack not 3 :NotLikeThis:
[2019-01-23 19:36] deadhour: Yeah, Exhaust kinda changes the math on things
[2019-01-23 19:36] amalloy: tarsiers are 5 permanent attack each if you do the honorable thing and get 4 ampos
[2019-01-23 19:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): in the context of ~~a full run~~ damage per turn it's 1 ~~A press~~ attack, but in the context of a single ~~level~~ burst it's 3 A presses 🤔
[2019-01-23 19:37] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): did you know you have to buy four 1dmg ampos to get one 5dmg tarsier?
[2019-01-23 19:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): so if i buy 4 ampos and 1 tarsier, i have a total of 9 attack. that's how the math works out, right?
[2019-01-23 19:38] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): yeah makes sense to me
[2019-01-23 19:40] winged: punching down doesn't feel that lighthearted
[2019-01-23 19:43] apooche: Cooledcannon has been a broken stair in this community for a long time
[2019-01-23 19:43] winged: then... why are they still here?
[2019-01-23 19:43] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): cooledcannon wasn't even involved here though?
[2019-01-23 19:44] winged: ```
i call it turn based starcraft with dominion elements but not the gay ones, with the magic elements but not the gay ones, with the chess wait hang on how is pris like chess again i forgot L
```

[2019-01-23 19:44] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): oh nvm I didn't see this
[2019-01-23 19:46] winged: I pointed out that this was basically homophobic, and then everyone chimed off of my 'homophobic' comment to make gay jokes
[2019-01-23 19:46] amalloy: @Awaclus it doesn't really matter if you "mean anything by it"
[2019-01-23 19:46] apooche: I don’t know Winged, mods not powerful enough I guess. Too much deleting of objectionable things but never actually addressing it
